page: Microsoft Azure EC16es_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECEV6 server family. It is built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor. The instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

A key architectural characteristic of the EC16es_v6 is the absence of local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external volumes, though the instance is designated as Premium Storage capable. There are no onboard GPUs, and the server does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. These hardware trade-offs position the server specifically for workloads that rely on externalized storage architectures and do not require hardware acceleration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density per dedicated vCPU makes this instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of paying for unneeded local storage or GPU hardware. The resource allocation profile is aligned with workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching systems, and enterprise applications that require substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources.
description: Microsoft Azure EC16es_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine within the ECEV6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable, meaning storage needs must be handled via external network-attached volumes. The absence of local storage and GPUs represents a design trade-off that optimizes the server for memory-heavy tasks without unnecessary hardware overhead. The EC16es_v6 is configured for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring high memory capacity relative to compute power.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated cores and one hundred twenty-eight gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
